name: BB+RSI Mean Reversion US500 M5
hypothesis: US500 M5 reverts to the BB mid when price closes beyond BB(20, 2.0) with RSI(2) at extremes, but only when volatility is active (BB-width above 30th percentile) and during US cash session (13:30-20:00 UTC). Outside these conditions the edge is dominated by trend drift.

entry_rules.long: close < bb20.lower AND rsi2 < 5 AND prev_close < bb20.lower
entry_rules.short: close > bb20.upper AND rsi2 > 95 AND prev_close > bb20.upper

notes: Different family from candidates 0/1. BB-width percentile and ADX ceiling are the key regime gates; plain BB+RSI without them historically produces zero-edge churn like gen40.
